notes: Future phase plan published to Intervals.icu so the full month is visible. These workouts are adaptive drafts: review feedback, Intervals form, weather, sleep, and family schedule before execution. Summer rule remains no indoor trainer. Friday PM is a high-value summer bike window when dry, not a default gym slot. Default is 1 gym session per week plus 4 bike touches; a fifth ride is optional only when it helps move build weeks into controlled green form without compromising recovery.

workouts.title: Full-Body Strength Maintenance
workouts.objective: Build real full-body strength while keeping lower-body fatigue controlled for the bike.
workouts.why_now: A lower-value weekday slot is better for gym work, preserving Friday PM for an outdoor bike session while still giving upper body a proper training dose.

targets.notes: 65-70 minutes. Lower body RPE 5-6 with 2-3 reps in reserve. Upper body RPE 6-7 with 1-2 reps in reserve. This is real strength work, not mobility, but no failure or soreness chasing.

centr_recommendation.primary: Centr full-body or upper/lower strength session, using the coach table as the prescription
alternatives: Strength for Sports upper-body plus lower-body basics
centr_recommendation.notes: Use the coach table as the prescription. Use Centr for demos or substitutions; keep upper body honest and lower body controlled.

gym_table.exercise: Dumbbell bench press or machine chest press

pre_interval_cues: This is real strength work. Keep lower body controlled and upper body honest.
during_workout_cues: Upper body can work harder than legs.
Use Centr only for matching exercise demos or substitutions.
execution_notes.if_too_easy: Add one upper-body accessory set, not heavy leg volume.
execution_notes.success_criteria: No leg heaviness the next morning.

workouts.title: Controlled Sweet Spot Rebuild
workouts.objective: Reintroduce sustained pressure below threshold without testing.
workouts.why_now: Friday PM is the best weekday bike window, and this controlled quality ride uses that availability without maximal testing.

targets.notes: Outdoor only. Do this only if legs are fresh. RPE cap 6. This is controlled green-zone build, not a test.

pre_interval_cues: Downgrade to endurance if sleep, legs, or weather are poor.
during_workout_cues: If RPE hits 7, ride endurance instead.
execution_notes.if_too_easy: Stay within target. Do not turn this into threshold.
execution_notes.if_too_hard: Do 3x10 minutes or convert to endurance.
execution_notes.success_criteria: Three controlled reps with no fade and RPE no higher than 6.

workouts.title: Aerobic Endurance With Fueling Practice
workouts.objective: Build endurance while practicing early fueling.
workouts.why_now: Longer aerobic ride after controlled quality, not another hard session.

targets.notes: Outdoor only. Keep 58-72% FTP. Eat early and steadily. This is the main aerobic load builder for the week.

steps.notes: Steady aerobic. Fuel from the first 30 minutes. Keep climbs controlled.

cooldown.notes: Ease down by feel; no need to force ultra-low watts.

pre_interval_cues: If Friday sweet spot or Saturday's easy ride felt costly, cap this at 90-120 minutes.
execution_notes.if_too_easy: Do not extend without review; this week should already reach light green.
execution_notes.if_too_hard: Shorten to 90-120 minutes if fatigue is clearly elevated; otherwise cap at 2h15-2h45.
execution_notes.success_criteria: Stable power, stable mood, no late bonk.

workouts.title: Easy Endurance Frequency Ride
workouts.objective: Add a fourth low-stress bike touch while keeping the week mostly aerobic.
workouts.why_now: This low-cost Saturday ride preserves frequency after Friday quality while keeping Sunday endurance protected.

targets.notes: Outdoor only. 70 minutes at 50-68% FTP. If rain blocks the ride, skip or do easy Centr mobility at home; do not add a second gym session.

cooldown.notes: Ease down by feel; no need to force ultra-low watts.

pre_interval_cues: Skip if the weekend created unusual fatigue.
during_workout_cues: Use this to add frequency, not stress.

workouts.title: Easy Aerobic Check
workouts.objective: Add low-stress aerobic work and check freshness.

targets.notes: Outdoor only if it fits easily and there is a clean 60-minute window. Ride easy endurance/low Z2 at about 55-68% FTP after a gentle ramp. Skip or move if rain blocks the ride.

warmup.notes: Start easy and ramp gradually into low endurance. The warmup should never feel harder than the main ride.

steps.notes: Easy endurance/low Z2. No hidden tempo.

cooldown.notes: Ease down below the main ride. Keep pressure light and let the legs unload.

pre_interval_cues: Skip if Tuesday gym leaves leg heaviness or if there is not a clean 60-minute outdoor window.
execution_notes.if_too_hard: If legs feel wrong before starting, rest instead. If already riding, turn home easy; do not turn this into a forced workout.

workouts.title: Optional Green-Zone Recovery Spin
workouts.objective: Add low-cost frequency only if Week 2 is being absorbed well.
workouts.why_now: The fitness graph needs some productive fatigue, but the safest addition is a short easy ride, not more intensity.

source_context.week_objective: Rebuild rhythm and aerobic consistency while nudging form into light green.

targets.notes: Optional outdoor ride. 60 minutes at 45-62% FTP. Drop first if sleep, legs, rain, or family schedule are not easy.

warmup.notes: Very easy by feel; do not chase an artificially low wattage.

steps.notes: Recovery endurance only. Keep it easier than normal endurance.

pre_interval_cues: Only do this if the week feels controlled and sleep is normal.
during_workout_cues: No tempo and no chasing average speed.
execution_notes.success_criteria: Adds fatigue gently without changing next week readiness.

workouts.title: Full-Body Strength Maintenance
workouts.objective: Maintain full-body strength with proper upper-body push/pull while keeping legs ready for the weekend.
workouts.why_now: A lower-value weekday slot is better for gym work, preserving Friday PM for the key outdoor bike session while still progressing gym strength.

targets.notes: 65-70 minutes. Default to 3 controlled sets for squat, hinge, and trunk work if legs are normal. Lower body RPE 5-6 with 2-3 reps in reserve, no failure. Upper body RPE 6-7 with 1-2 reps in reserve. Reduce lower-body volume first if soreness or bike fatigue appears.

centr_recommendation.primary: Centr full-body or upper/lower strength session, using the coach table as the prescription
alternatives: Multi-Gym Strength upper-body plus lower-body basics
centr_recommendation.notes: This is full-body maintenance. If the selected Centr workout is harder than the coach table, reduce lower-body sets first and keep upper-body work controlled.

gym_table.notes: Moderate load, clean hamstring tension, no grinding.

gym_table.exercise: Dumbbell bench press or machine chest press

pre_interval_cues: Reduce volume if legs are not fresh.
during_workout_cues: Upper body can work harder than legs.
execution_notes.if_too_easy: If legs are normal, keep the 3 controlled sets and add one upper-body accessory set only if time remains.
execution_notes.if_too_hard: Cut squat and hinge to 2 sets, then fill remaining time with upper body, trunk, mobility, or easy cardio rather than ending underdosed.
execution_notes.success_criteria: No soreness that affects the weekend.

workouts.title: Controlled Over-Under Without Testing
workouts.objective: Build threshold control without doing an FTP test.
workouts.why_now: Second build week can include one harder sustained-power stimulus if feedback is stable, and Friday PM is the best weekday window to execute it well.

targets.notes: Outdoor only. Execute only if freshness is good. This is the single hard stimulus of the week, not an FTP test.

pre_interval_cues: Skip or downgrade if Week 2 felt costly.
during_workout_cues: If cadence or breathing collapses, stop the set.
execution_notes.if_too_easy: Do not add a fourth set without coach review.

workouts.title: Long Aerobic Endurance
workouts.objective: Extend aerobic durability with stable fueling.
workouts.why_now: Builds endurance after the weekly quality day without extra intensity.

targets.notes: Outdoor only. 58-72% FTP. Main aerobic durability load. No tempo finish unless explicitly reviewed.

cooldown.notes: Ease down by feel; no need to force ultra-low watts.

execution_notes.if_too_easy: Do not extend without review; this week is already a controlled overload.

workouts.title: Easy Aerobic Support Ride
workouts.objective: Preserve bike frequency and add easy aerobic volume without another hard stimulus.
workouts.why_now: This low-cost Saturday ride supports frequency after Friday quality while preserving the Sunday long ride.

targets.notes: Outdoor only. 70 minutes mostly 50-68% FTP. If rain blocks the ride, use easy mobility at home; do not add a second gym day.

steps.notes: Endurance only. Smooth cadence, no pressure.

cooldown.notes: Ease down by feel; no need to force ultra-low watts.

pre_interval_cues: Only ride if weekend fatigue is normal.
during_workout_cues: Keep it easy enough to protect the next rides.
execution_notes.success_criteria: You add volume without fatigue debt.

workouts.title: Endurance With Cadence Skills
workouts.objective: Add easy aerobic time with low-cost cadence work.
workouts.why_now: Maintains frequency without another intensity day.

targets.notes: Outdoor only. Keep power easy during cadence work.

cooldown.notes: Ease down by feel; no need to force ultra-low watts.

pre_interval_cues: Cadence work should not become intensity.

workouts.title: Optional Absorption Spin
workouts.objective: Hold productive green-zone fatigue without adding another hard session.
workouts.why_now: If Build Week 2 is being absorbed, a short easy ride can keep frequency high before deload.

source_context.week_objective: Build durable sustained power while keeping fatigue controlled.

targets.notes: Optional outdoor ride. 60 minutes at 45-62% FTP. Drop first if form is too negative, RPE is high, or sleep is poor.

warmup.notes: Very easy by feel; do not chase an artificially low wattage.

pre_interval_cues: Only do this if you are absorbing the week well.
during_workout_cues: Keep it below normal endurance pressure.
execution_notes.success_criteria: You enter deload pleasantly tired, not depleted.

workouts.title: Deload Mobility And Light Strength
workouts.objective: Start deload week with mobility and very light strength.
workouts.why_now: The goal is absorption, not more training load; Friday PM stays available for an easy outdoor ride if weather and fatigue allow.

targets.notes: RPE 3-4. Centr yoga, Pilates, mobility, or easy core preferred.

centr_recommendation.primary: Centr Pilates Fusion - Week 1 Day 4 - Pilates & mobility - 45 min
alternatives: HYROX Recover - Week 1 Day 1 - Mobility - 20 min - Bodyweight
HYROX Recover - Week 1 Day 3 - Mobility - 20 min - Bodyweight
centr_recommendation.notes: Deload week: this should make you feel fresher, not trained.

gym_table.exercise: Centr recovery mobility, yoga, or Pilates

workouts.title: Deload Easy Spin With Optional Openers
workouts.objective: Keep legs moving during deload without accumulating fatigue.

targets.notes: Outdoor only. Optional openers only if legs are clearly fresh.

steps.notes: Optional opener. Skip if not fresh.

pre_interval_cues: Skip openers if fatigue is present.
execution_notes.if_too_hard: Skip the openers and ride easy only; stop early only if safety or symptoms require it.
execution_notes.success_criteria: Legs feel better after than before.

workouts.title: Deload Easy Endurance Or Rest
workouts.objective: Optional easy ride during deload if freshness is improving.

targets.notes: Optional. 50-65% FTP. Rest instead if life schedule or weather makes this costly.

pre_interval_cues: Rest is acceptable and may be better.
execution_notes.if_too_easy: Do not extend much during deload.

workouts.title: Deload Recovery Spin Or Mobility
workouts.objective: Keep frequency during deload with a very easy aerobic touch.
workouts.why_now: Friday PM is available for riding, but deload logic keeps this as an easy spin only if it improves recovery.

targets.notes: Outdoor only. 60 minutes easy at 45-60% FTP. If rain or fatigue makes this costly, skip and use 20-30 minutes Centr mobility.

warmup.notes: Very easy by feel; do not chase an artificially low wattage.

pre_interval_cues: This is optional. Rest is acceptable during deload.
during_workout_cues: Keep it calmer than normal endurance.
Stop if legs feel worse after 15 minutes.
execution_notes.if_too_hard: Stop early or do mobility only.
execution_notes.success_criteria: You feel looser and more rested after.

workouts.title: Easy Readiness Check
workouts.objective: Check readiness at the end of deload without testing FTP.
workouts.why_now: Determines whether the next phase can progress.

targets.notes: Outdoor only. Easy with 3 short controlled efforts only if fresh.

cooldown.notes: Ease down by feel; no need to force ultra-low watts.

during_workout_cues: This checks feel, not max power.
execution_notes.if_too_easy: Good. Save it for next phase.
execution_notes.success_criteria: Controlled efforts feel smooth and leave freshness.
